name: Lexicle
based_on: Wordle (Josh Wardle / NYT) — one daily five-letter word, six guesses, green/yellow/gray letter feedback, an on-screen keyboard, streaks, and a shareable emoji-grid result.
summary: A clean daily word-guessing game: six tries to find the day's five-letter word, with green/yellow/gray tile feedback, a color-coded on-screen keyboard, a win/loss stats modal, and one-tap emoji-grid sharing.
sections: 6x5 guess board with flip-reveal tile animations
On-screen QWERTY keyboard that recolors keys by best-known status
Daily-word logic (deterministic word-of-the-day from the date, same for everyone)
Stats modal: games played, win %, current streak, max streak, guess-distribution bars
Share button producing the spoiler-free emoji grid to clipboard
How-to-play modal with example tiles, plus localStorage so refreshing keeps your progress
setup_questions: What should the game be named, and is there a theme for the word list (general English, movies, food, or your own custom word pack)?
What accent color should the correct (green) tiles use to match your brand?
What hashtag or site name should appear when players share their result grid?
Should it be a fresh word every day, or an unlimited 'play again' practice mode?
photo_terms: green and yellow color swatch palette
